channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Elderly-Contextual Data Augmentation via Speech Synthesis for Elderly ASR
item.description: arXiv:2604.24770v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite recent progress in automatic speech recognition (ASR), elderly ASR (EASR) remains challenging due to limited training data and the distinct acoustic and linguistic characteristics of elderly speech. In this work, we address data scarcity in EA...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, model, audio, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Large Language Models Explore by Latent Distilling
item.description: arXiv:2604.24927v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating diverse responses is crucial for test-time scaling of large language models (LLMs), yet standard stochastic sampling mostly yields surface-level lexical variation, limiting semantic exploration. In this paper, we propose Exploratory Samplin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, paper, large language model, RAG, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GAIA-v2-LILT: Multilingual Adaptation of Agent Benchmark beyond Translation
item.description: arXiv:2604.24929v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent benchmarks remain largely English-centric, while their multilingual versions are often built with machine translation (MT) and limited post-editing. We argue that, for agentic tasks, this minimal workflow can easily break benchmark validity thro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, alignment, experiment, context, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Survey on LLM-based Conversational User Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2604.24977v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: User simulation has long played a vital role in computer science due to its potential to support a wide range of applications. Language, as the primary medium of human communication, forms the foundation of social interaction and behavior. Consequentl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, paper, large language model, framework, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Don\'t Stop Early: Scalable Enterprise Deep Research with Controlled Information Flow and Evidence-Aware Termination
item.description: arXiv:2604.24978v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enterprise deep research often fails to produce decision-ready reports due to uneven information coverage, context explosion, and premature stopping. We propose a scalable Enterprise Deep Research (EDR) architecture to address these failures. Our syst...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, RAG, research, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Why Does Reinforcement Learning Generalize? A Feature-Level Mechanistic Study of Post-Training in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.25011v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL)-based post-training often improves the reasoning performance of large language models (LLMs) beyond the training domain, while supervised fine-tuning (SFT) frequently leads to general capabilities forgetting. However, the m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, fine-tuning, RAG, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An Intelligent Fault Diagnosis Method for General Aviation Aircraft Based on Multi-Fidelity Digital Twin and FMEA Knowledge Enhancement
item.description: arXiv:2604.22777v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fault diagnosis of general aviation aircraft faces challenges including scarce real fault data, diverse fault types, and weak fault signatures. This paper proposes an intelligent fault diagnosis framework based on multi-fidelity digital twin, integrat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, paper, large language model, framework, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Systematic Approach for Large Language Models Debugging
item.description: arXiv:2604.23027v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become central to modern AI workflows, powering applications from open-ended text generation to complex agent-based reasoning. However, debugging these models remains a persistent challenge due to their opaque and pro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, paper, large language model, fine-tuning, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Decoupled Human-in-the-Loop System for Controlled Autonomy in Agentic Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2604.23049v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents are increasingly deployed to execute tasks and make decisions within agentic workflows, introducing new requirements for safe and controlled autonomy. Prior work has established the importance of human oversight for ensuring transparency, ac...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, framework, model, alignment, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Don't Make the LLM Read the Graph: Make the Graph Think
item.description: arXiv:2604.23057v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate whether explicit belief graphs improve LLM performance in cooperative multi-agent reasoning. Through 3,000+ controlled trials across four LLM families in the cooperative card game Hanabi, we establish four findings. First, integration a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, analysis, reasoning, model, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Analytica: Soft Propositional Reasoning for Robust and Scalable LLM-Driven Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.23072v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly tasked with complex real-world analysis (e.g., in financial forecasting, scientific discovery), yet their reasoning suffers from stochastic instability and lacks a verifiable, compositional structure....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, RAG, framework, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GCA-BULF: A Bottom-Up Framework for Short-Term Load Forecasting Using Grouped Critical Appliances
item.description: arXiv:2604.24766v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the rise of time-of-use and tiered electricity pricing, energy consumers are encouraged to adopt peak-shifting strategies by automatically controlling high-power appliances. These help lower energy costs while enhancing the power grid's stability...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, framework, experiment, context, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Intrinsic Mutual Information as a Modulator for Preference Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2604.24804v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Offline preference optimization methods, such as Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), offer significant advantages in aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with human values. However, achieving optimal performance with these methods typically involve...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, framework, RAG, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Comparative Analysis on the Performance of Upper Confidence Bound Algorithms in Adaptive Deep Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2604.24810v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Edge computing environments impose strict constraints on energy consumption and latency, making the deployment of deep neural networks a significant challenge. Therefore, smart and adaptive inference strategies that dynamically balance computational c...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, framework, arxiv, experiment, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On the Existence of an Inverse Solution for Preference-Based Reductions in Argumentation
item.description: arXiv:2604.22958v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Preference-based argumentation frameworks (PAFs) extend Dung's approach to abstract argumentation (AAFs) by encoding preferences over arguments. Such preferences control the transformation of attacks into defeats, and different approaches to doing so ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, context, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Query-Efficient Quantum Approximate Optimization via Graph-Conditioned Trust Regions
item.description: arXiv:2604.24803v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In low-depth implementations of the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA), the dominant cost is often the number of objective evaluations rather than circuit depth. We introduce a graph-conditioned trust-region method for reducing this que...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: minAction.net: Energy-First Neural Architecture Design -- From Biological Principles to Systematic Validation
item.description: arXiv:2604.24805v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern machine learning optimizes for accuracy without explicitly accounting for internal computational cost, even though physical and biological systems operate under intrinsic energy constraints. We evaluate energy-aware learning across 2,203 experi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, vision, research, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Comparative Study of Bending Analysis using Physics-Informed Neural Networks and Numerical Dynamic Deflection in Perforated nanobeam
item.description: arXiv:2604.24768v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this chapter, we investigate the bending behavior of a perforated nanobeam subjected to sinusoidal loading using an efficient and computationally robust Physics-Informed Functional Link Constrained Framework with Domain Mapping (DFL-TFC) method. Ou...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, analysis, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Power of Power Law: Asymmetry Enables Compositional Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.22951v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language data follows a power-law distribution, with most knowledge and skills appearing at very low frequency. While a common intuition suggests that reweighting or curating data towards a uniform distribution may help models better learn the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, analysis, arxiv, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Liquid Neural Network Models for Natural Gas Spot Price Time-Series Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2604.24788v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural gas is undoubtedly an essential component of the global energy system. Accurate short-term forecasting of natural gas price is challenging due to pronounced volatility driven by seasonal demand patterns, geopolitical developments, and shifting...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
